title: Prunus zhengheensis
intro: Prunus zhengheensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae, commonly known as the Zhenghe apricot or apricot rose. The fruit of Prunus zhengheensis has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects. The bioactive constituents of Prunus zhengheensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Prunus zhengheensis is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, decoctions, and infusions, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Prunus zhengheensis is a species of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae). It is a type of cherry tree native to China. The genus Prunus includes various species of stone fruits, such as cherries and plums. Prunus zhengheensis is closely related to other species within the Prunus genus. It is classified as a deciduous shrub or small tree.
